title: Sampling for Flow Cytometry (FCM)
author: Sarah Giuliani
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.f25bqg6

steps.title: Advance preparation
steps.instruction: Advance preparation: for each sample to be collected, aliquot 900 µl of seawater (artificial seawater salts or complete sterile medium) into a labeled 1.2 ml cryovial. Adjust the dilution if necessary for your samples (this protocol uses a 10-fold dilution; if your sample is very concentrated and/or you are extremely sample-limited, you can dilute more; if your sample is dilute (<1E6 cells/ml), then you might want to dilute less or not at all). If your samples are not seawater-based, substitute an appropriate diluent of the same ionic strength (e.g. sterile medium, sterile freshwater salts).

steps.title: Collect sample
steps.instruction: Collect sample: add 100 µl of sample into cryovial containing 900 µl of seawater. Keep cryovial cap clean and sterile.

steps.title: Preserve/fix sample
steps.instruction: Preserve/fix sample: In the chemical fume hood, lay out cryovial caps (inside cap facing up) and use the repeator pipet to add 5 µl of glutaraldehyde (25%) to each cap. Cap each tube and mix well by inverting a few times. Incubate for 10 minutes in the dark. Final concentration of glutaraldehyde is 0.125%.

steps.title: Flash-freeze tubes in liquid nitrogen and...
steps.instruction: Flash-freeze tubes in liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C until analysis.
